responses.text: The target article reports on a supposed cross-generational conflict in which Generation Z TikTok users tried to "cancel" the rapper Eminem over violent lyrics, prompting fierce backlash from millennial fans.
The controversy traces back to a single February 2021 TikTok post by a user who criticized Eminem's 2010 song "Love the Way You Lie" for its depictions of domestic violence.
News outlets amplified this single post into a narrative of an organized "Gen Z" cancellation campaign, which triggered a wave of defensive posts from older fans.
The most widely circulated viral video in the article—a millennial fan "rapping beautifully" to a Dr. Dre beat in defense of Eminem—was actually widely mocked across social media as "cringeworthy."
The New York Post's framing takes this millennial's video as a serious "coup de grâce," omitting the context that her video became an object of widespread ridicule and parody on TikTok and Twitter.
Eminem himself responded to the chatter by releasing an animated lyric video for his song "Tone Deaf," leaning into the controversy.

Ultimately, the event reveals more about how media outlets can manufacture a "culture war" from a few isolated social media posts than it does about any serious demographic movement to cancel the artist.
